plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for higher income exposure, favoring steady returns.
Trim QQQ to 10% due to its downtrend, reducing exposure to weakness.
Initiate a new position in MSFT at 10% as a top-quality long setup, adding stability and growth potential.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5%/month, focusing on lower drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but potential upside is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing risk management.
Trim QQQI to 15% and QQQ to 10% due to their downtrends, reducing exposure to weakness.
Initiate new positions in NVDU and MSFT at 10% each, adding strong quality tech setups.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month by blending growth and risk management. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech, aiming for controlled growth with moderate drawdown risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate while maintaining significant broad market exposure.
Trim QQQI and QQQ to 10% each due to their downtrends, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Maintain SOXL at 0% but watch for trend reversal, as aggressive mode allows re-entry on strength.
Initiate new positions in NVDU and MSFT at 15% each, concentrating in high-scoring long setups.
Keep SQQQ on watch as a potential hedge if tech weakness intensifies, allowing for quick tactical entry.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into leverage and high-scoring setups. This approach significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown risk. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and strict stop discipline is crucial to manage rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital for income-focused assets.
Increase QQQI to 30% to significantly boost monthly distribution potential.
Trim QQQ, SMH, and CRWD to 5% each to reduce growth and high-beta exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs, prioritizing low drawdown and stable income.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month by prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. It rotates into income-focused ETFs and reduces high-beta exposure. While aiming for stability, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
